diagrams.title: Cloud Service Models - Responsibility Matrix
diagrams.description: Shows the division of responsibility between cloud provider and user across IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS
elements: IaaS: Provider handles Physical→Virtualization, User handles OS→Applications
PaaS: Provider handles Physical→Runtime, User handles Applications
SaaS: Provider handles everything, User just uses application
diagrams.purpose: Understanding who manages what in each service model, crucial for exam questions on cloud responsibilities

diagrams.title: Cloud Deployment Models
diagrams.description: Visual representation of Public, Private, Hybrid, and Community cloud architectures
elements: Public: Shared data center across organizations
Private: Dedicated data center for one organization
Hybrid: Private connected to public cloud
Community: Shared data center for specific community
diagrams.purpose: Differentiating between deployment models and understanding use cases for each type

diagrams.title: Cloud Computing Working Architecture
diagrams.description: End-to-end flow of how a cloud request is processed and fulfilled
elements: User Request → Web Portal/API → Cloud Management Layer → Virtualization (Hypervisor) → Resource Pooling → Data Center Resources → Service Delivery → Billing/Monitoring
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the complete lifecycle of a cloud request from user to delivered service

diagrams.title: NIST Essential Characteristics of Cloud Computing
diagrams.description: Five core characteristics that define cloud computing
elements: 1. On-Demand Self-Service 2. Broad Network Access 3. Resource Pooling 4. Rapid Elasticity 5. Measured Service
diagrams.purpose: Memorizing the five NIST characteristics, fundamental for understanding cloud computing definition
